headline: Mongolia FIRST BASELINE CONFIRM at 48.4 / Functional — UN High Commissioner Türk's official visit (May 10-11, first visit in 26 years) provides unusually high-quality institutional primary-source evidence. Positive: first human rights defenders law in Asia-Pacific region (enacted, not signaled); business and human rights action plan; nuclear-weapons-free zone; death penalty abolition cited as regional models; exemplary National Human Rights Commission; active civil society. Concerns: corruption described as 'biggest bottleneck' by civil society; stronger anti-corruption measures 'clearly necessary' per Türk; implementation and enforcement gap noted; digital safety/online laws need human rights framing. UN Independent Expert on SOGI country visit scheduled May 18-29 — forthcoming EQU evidence flagged as 30-day forward signal. Clean reconstruction at the published Functional band midpoint; medium confidence given first-baseline nature and rich evidence anchor.
key_evidence: May 10-11 2026 (in-window): UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk conducted official visit — first by UN HC in nearly 26 years. Press conference May 11.
OHCHR press statement (in-window): Mongolia adopted the first law for the protection of human rights defenders in the Asia-Pacific region — concrete enacted SYS/EQU signal.

OHCHR (in-window): Mongolia developed one of the first business and human rights action plans in the region; strong human rights framework in law; exemplary National Human Rights Commission; active civil society.
OHCHR (in-window): Nuclear-weapons-free zone status and abolition of the death penalty cited as regional models — concrete BND/EQU enacted signals.

OHCHR (in-window, civil society representative to Türk): 'corruption is the biggest bottleneck to the development of the country.' Türk: stronger anti-corruption measures 'clearly necessary.' ACC/SYS dimension concern.
OHCHR (in-window): Implementation and enforcement gap noted by civil society — impatience for stronger operational follow-through on adopted laws.
OHCHR (in-window): Digital technology and social media impact on youth; proposed online safety laws require human rights framing.
UN Independent Expert on SOGI country visit scheduled May 18-29 (call for inputs posted) — forthcoming EQU evidence.

evidence.source: OHCHR — UN Human Rights Chief to conduct official visits to Mongolia and Republic of Korea 10-14 May
evidence.finding: First UN HC visit to Mongolia in 26 years; institutional engagement signal.

evidence.source: OHCHR — Press conference by UN High Commissioner Volker Türk
evidence.finding: Human rights defenders law (first in region); business and human rights action plan; nuclear-free zone; death penalty abolition; corruption concerns; implementation gaps.

evidence.source: OHCHR — Mongolia's history of dialogue and diplomacy is a strong foundation for the future
evidence.finding: Diplomatic tradition praised as regional model; BND structural anchor.

evidence.source: UN News — Human rights in Mongolia
evidence.finding: Multi-source confirmation of human rights progress + corruption bottleneck; UN system engagement.

evidence.source: OHCHR — Call for inputs to advance country visit Mongolia UN Independent Expert SOGI 18-29
evidence.finding: Forthcoming SOGI country visit May 18-29 — EQU dimension evidence forthcoming; institutional engagement signal.

evidence.source: UNICEF Mongolia — UN expert calls on Mongolia to adopt law protecting human rights defenders
evidence.finding: Pre-window context: UN expert advocacy preceded the regional-first law's adoption; trajectory signal.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.0 sustained at published. A1 detection: National Human Rights Commission noted as exemplary; civil society active; OHCHR engagement provides external monitoring channel. Anchor 3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.0 sustained at published. E1 affective resonance: human rights defenders law signals state-level empathic regard for protected populations; E2 protected scope: business and human rights action plan extends concern to economic-actor harm pathway. Anchor 3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.0 sustained at published. AC1 responsiveness: adopted laws signal action capacity; AC3 implementation gap noted (impatience for stronger enforcement) caps upward movement. Anchor 3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 sustained at published. EQ1 universality: human rights defenders law expands protected scope; EQ2 protected categories: SOGI visit forthcoming May 18-29 will test EQ score; current implementation gaps and SOGI-related concerns (pre-visit) maintain anchor 2-3 boundary at 2.5. Conservative hold pending SOGI visit evidence.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.0 sustained at published. B1 sustainability: diplomatic tradition praised as regional foundation; B3 scope clarity: nuclear-weapons-free zone status; B5 multilateral engagement: first UN HC visit in 26 years sustains anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.0 sustained at published. AB1 harm-acknowledgment: civil society freely articulated corruption concerns to Türk in country; AB4 accountability-to-external-norms: invited UN HC visit and SOGI Expert visit; corruption bottleneck caps upward movement. Anchor 3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 sustained at published. S2 long-term-impact: human rights defenders law and business-and-human-rights action plan create structural protection mechanisms; S4 structural critique: Türk articulated need for stronger anti-corruption measures; gap-flagging by external observer provides structural-reform reference. Anchor 3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.0 sustained at published. I1 consistency: enacted laws (death penalty abolition, nuclear-free zone, human rights defenders law) demonstrate values-action alignment; I3 follow-through: implementation gaps noted by civil society temper upward movement. Anchor 3 sustained.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to 48.4 = published. Sub-threshold drift (0.0). Functional band sustained. Not a boundary case (>5pt from band boundaries).

negativeSignals: Corruption described as 'biggest bottleneck' to development by civil society; implementation and enforcement gap on adopted laws; proposed online safety/digital laws require human rights framing; SOGI dimension evidence pending the May 18-29 visit (could surface concerns).
positiveSignals: First UN HC visit in 26 years (institutional engagement signal); human rights defenders law — first in Asia-Pacific region (concrete enacted); business and human rights action plan (regional first); nuclear-weapons-free zone; death penalty abolition; exemplary National Human Rights Commission; active civil society; SOGI country visit scheduled (institutional engagement continuing); diplomatic tradition praised as regional foundation.
sector_context: Asia-Pacific / East Asia cluster. Mongolia is the first Asia-Pacific country with a human rights defenders law — regional-leadership signal. The 48.4 (Functional) published score is robust under the high-quality OHCHR primary-source evidence. The SOGI visit May 18-29 will generate additional EQU evidence — 30-day forward signal flagged for next assessment cycle.
open_actions_resolved: First baseline established with high-quality OHCHR primary-source evidence
Human rights defenders law documented as concrete SYS/EQU enacted signal
Corruption bottleneck documented as ACC/SYS concern
SOGI visit forthcoming flagged as 30-day forward signal
open_actions_carried: May 18-29: UN Independent Expert on SOGI country visit — EQU dimension evidence forthcoming
Watch: digital safety/online laws — human rights framing
Watch: implementation and enforcement progress on adopted laws
